{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Cashier",
  "description": "Tonia Pharmacy Limited was incorporated in the year 2003 (RC: 497235) to carry on business as a pharmaceutical company. Since inception, the company has grown to become one of the reputable pharmacies in Nigeria, with several branches located within FCT ABUJA.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Cashier

Job Location:&nbsp;All Branches, Nigeria
Department: Finance / Retail Operations
Reports To: Branch Manager / Superintendent Pharmacist.
Organization: Tonia Pharmacy Limited &amp; Superstore

Job Summary


	The Cashier is responsible for handling all financial transactions within the pharmacy and superstore in a timely, accurate, and professional manner.
	The role involves receiving payments, issuing receipts, maintaining cash accountability, and ensuring excellent customer service while complying with company policies and regulatory standards.


Key Responsibilities
Transaction Processing:


	Receive and process payments (cash, POS, transfers) for goods and services.
	Accurately issue receipts and invoices to customers.
	Ensure correct pricing, discounts, and promotions are applied.
	Balance cash register at the beginning and end of each shift.


Customer Service:


	Greet customers warmly and attend to them in a professional manner.
	Respond to inquiries regarding products, prices, and store policies.
	Handle customer complaints politely and escalate when necessary.
	Maintain a friendly and welcoming checkout environment.


Cash Handling &amp; Accountability:


	Maintain proper custody of cash and prevent shortages or overages.
	Perform daily cash reconciliation and report discrepancies immediately.
	Ensure all transactions are properly recorded in the system.
	Safeguard POS machines and other financial tools.


Sales Support:


	Support sales activities by promoting ongoing offers and products.
	Cross-sell and upsell pharmacy and supermarket items where appropriate.
	Assist customers in locating products when necessary.


Record Keeping &amp; Reporting:


	Maintain accurate transaction records and daily sales reports.
	Prepare end-of-day financial summaries.
	Assist in stock documentation when required.


Compliance &amp; Controls:


	Adhere strictly to company financial policies and procedures.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and regulatory standards.
	Prevent fraud, theft, or misuse of company resources.


Store Operations Support:


	Maintain cleanliness and organization of the cashier area.
	Assist in stock arrangement and shelf monitoring when required.
	Support inventory counts and audits when assigned.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Accuracy in transactions (zero or minimal errors)
	Daily cash balancing compliance
	Customer satisfaction ratings
	Speed and efficiency of service
	Reduction in cash discrepancies
	Upselling contribution to sales.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Minimum of HND / BSc Degree&nbsp;(additional qualifications are an advantage)
	Proven experience as a cashier or in a retail environment (pharmacy experience is an added advantage)
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles
	Familiarity with POS systems and basic computer skills
	Strong numerical and analytical skills.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	High level of integrity and honesty
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Ability to work under pressure
	Good customer service orientation
	Time management and multitasking abilities.


Working Conditions:


	Requires standing for long periods
	May involve weekend and shift work
	Fast-paced retail and healthcare environment.


Additional Requirements:


	Professional appearance and conduct
	Ability to handle confidential financial information
	Team player with a positive attitude.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@toniapharmacy.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-03",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Tonia Pharmacy Limited"
    
  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

  },

      "maxValue": 0,

    }
  }
}



Cashier Job at Tonia Pharmacy Limited




        
    

        
        Tonia Pharmacy Limited was incorporated in the year 2003 (RC: 497235) to carry on business as a pharmaceutical company. Since inception, the company has grown to become one of the reputable pharmacies in Nigeria, with several branches located within FCT ABUJA.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Cashier

Job Location: All Branches, Nigeria
Department: Finance / Retail Operations
Reports To: Branch Manager / Superintendent Pharmacist.
Organization: Tonia Pharmacy Limited & Superstore

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Cashier is responsible for handling all financial transactions within the pharmacy and superstore in a timely, accurate, and professional manner.
	The role involves receiving payments, issuing receipts, maintaining cash accountability, and ensuring excellent customer service while complying with company policies and regulatory standards.


Key Responsibilities
Transaction Processing:


	Receive and process payments (cash, POS, transfers) for goods and services.
	Accurately issue receipts and invoices to customers.
	Ensure correct pricing, discounts, and promotions are applied.
	Balance cash register at the beginning and end of each shift.


Customer Service:


	Greet customers warmly and attend to them in a professional manner.
	Respond to inquiries regarding products, prices, and store policies.
	Handle customer complaints politely and escalate when necessary.
	Maintain a friendly and welcoming checkout environment.


Cash Handling & Accountability:


	Maintain proper custody of cash and prevent shortages or overages.
	Perform daily cash reconciliation and report discrepancies immediately.
	Ensure all transactions are properly recorded in the system.
	Safeguard POS machines and other financial tools.


Sales Support:


	Support sales activities by promoting ongoing offers and products.
	Cross-sell and upsell pharmacy and supermarket items where appropriate.
	Assist customers in locating products when necessary.


Record Keeping & Reporting:


	Maintain accurate transaction records and daily sales reports.
	Prepare end-of-day financial summaries.
	Assist in stock documentation when required.


Compliance & Controls:


	Adhere strictly to company financial policies and procedures.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and regulatory standards.
	Prevent fraud, theft, or misuse of company resources.


Store Operations Support:


	Maintain cleanliness and organization of the cashier area.
	Assist in stock arrangement and shelf monitoring when required.
	Support inventory counts and audits when assigned.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Accuracy in transactions (zero or minimal errors)
	Daily cash balancing compliance
	Customer satisfaction ratings
	Speed and efficiency of service
	Reduction in cash discrepancies
	Upselling contribution to sales.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Minimum of HND / BSc Degree (additional qualifications are an advantage)
	Proven experience as a cashier or in a retail environment (pharmacy experience is an added advantage)
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles
	Familiarity with POS systems and basic computer skills
	Strong numerical and analytical skills.


Skills & Competencies:


	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	High level of integrity and honesty
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Ability to work under pressure
	Good customer service orientation
	Time management and multitasking abilities.


Working Conditions:


	Requires standing for long periods
	May involve weekend and shift work
	Fast-paced retail and healthcare environment.


Additional Requirements:


	Professional appearance and conduct
	Ability to handle confidential financial information
	Team player with a positive attitude.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Clerk",
  "description": "Queens Clinic Abuja -&nbsp;We are your healthcare provider offering consultations and treatments across various specialties. Our services have been crafted to meet most of your medical needs. Our Diagnostics service facility is designed to give easy access, timely, and high-quality diagnostics care in a safe and secure environment. Our professional Doctors and Nurses are well trained to deliver medical treatments with care and good medical ethics.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Clerk

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Ensure that financial transactions of the company are accurately captured, classified and posted with relevant approvals and coding instructions


Job Functions / Responsibilities


	Receivable management
	Payables management
	Ensure accurate and timely invoicing for clients and patients
	Perform regular reviews of the ledgers and other books of entries and ensure relevant reconciliation.
	Ensure confidentiality and safe keeping of accounting records and documents.
	Adhere to financial and accounting control procedures of the company.
	Carry out monthly Tax Returns (PAYE, VAT, Withholding Tax e. t. c.)
	Ensure timely pension remittance.
	Weekly/Monthly Cash Plan
	Reconciliation reports.
	Weekly Reports (which includes Receivables Summary, Payables Summary)
	Monthly Income Statement, Statement of financial Position, Analysis of Expenses, Financial Summary etc.
	POS cashier.


Requirements / Qualifications


	OND qualification in Accountancy.
	0 - 1 year of working experience
	Applicant must live in Abuja.
	Candidate must be able to demonstrate the use of accounting software such as quickbook
	Must be able to use MS. word such as Excel etc and other accounting software at a professional level.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their&nbsp;Applications to:&nbsp;glory.queensclinicabuja@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Account Clerk Job at Queens Clinic Abuja




        
    

        
        Queens Clinic Abuja - We are your healthcare provider offering consultations and treatments across various specialties. Our services have been crafted to meet most of your medical needs. Our Diagnostics service facility is designed to give easy access, timely, and high-quality diagnostics care in a safe and secure environment. Our professional Doctors and Nurses are well trained to deliver medical treatments with care and good medical ethics.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Clerk

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Ensure that financial transactions of the company are accurately captured, classified and posted with relevant approvals and coding instructions


Job Functions / Responsibilities


	Receivable management
	Payables management
	Ensure accurate and timely invoicing for clients and patients
	Perform regular reviews of the ledgers and other books of entries and ensure relevant reconciliation.
	Ensure confidentiality and safe keeping of accounting records and documents.
	Adhere to financial and accounting control procedures of the company.
	Carry out monthly Tax Returns (PAYE, VAT, Withholding Tax e. t. c.)
	Ensure timely pension remittance.
	Weekly/Monthly Cash Plan
	Reconciliation reports.
	Weekly Reports (which includes Receivables Summary, Payables Summary)
	Monthly Income Statement, Statement of financial Position, Analysis of Expenses, Financial Summary etc.
	POS cashier.


Requirements / Qualifications


	OND qualification in Accountancy.
	0 - 1 year of working experience
	Applicant must live in Abuja.
	Candidate must be able to demonstrate the use of accounting software such as quickbook
	Must be able to use MS. word such as Excel etc and other accounting software at a professional level.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Mechanical Engineer",
  "description": "Global Linkers Limited - Our client in the Pharmaceutical Manufacturing industry, is&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mechanical Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Calabar, Cross River
Employment Type:

Job Summary


	They are serching for a Mechanical Engineer who will beresponsible for the installation, maintenance, and optimization of mechanical systems and equipment within the pharmaceutical manufacturing facility.
	The role ensures efficient operation of production machinery, utilities, and support systems in compliance with safety standards and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) requirements.


Key Roles and Responsibilities


	Install, maintain, and troubleshoot mechanical equipment used in pharmaceutical production (e.g., mixers, granulators, compressors, HVAC systems).
	Ensure all mechanical systems operate efficiently to support uninterrupted production activities.
	Develop and implement preventive and corrective maintenance schedules.
	Monitor equipment performance and recommend improvements to enhance efficiency and reduce downtime.
	Ensure compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) and safety standards in all engineering activities.
	Perform routine inspections of machinery and utilities to identify potential faults.
	Collaborate with Production, Quality Assurance, and Quality Control teams to ensure equipment meets operational and quality requirements.
	Maintain accurate maintenance records, logs, and documentation.
	Supervise and guide technicians and maintenance staff.
	Ensure proper calibration and functionality of mechanical systems supporting production.
	Support installation and commissioning of new equipment and systems.


Qualifications and Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree (B.Eng./B.Sc.) in Mechanical Engineering or related field.
	3&ndash;7 years&rsquo; experience in mechanical engineering, preferably in pharmaceutical, FMCG, or manufacturing environment.
	Knowledge of industrial machinery, HVAC systems, and utility systems.
	Familiarity with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) and safety standards.
	Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.
	Good communication and teamwork abilities.
	Professional certification (e.g., COREN) is an added advantage.


Key Competencies:


	Equipment maintenance and reliability
	Troubleshooting and fault diagnosis
	Preventive maintenance planning
	Safety and compliance awareness
	Team collaboration
	Technical documentation.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;apply@globallinkersltd.com&nbsp;using &quot;Mechanical Engineer -Calabar&quot;&nbsp;as the subjet of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-04",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Global Linkers Limited"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "196909"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Mechanical Engineer Job at Global Linkers Limited




        
    

        
        Global Linkers Limited - Our client in the Pharmaceutical Manufacturing industry, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mechanical Engineer

Job Location: Calabar, Cross River
Employment Type:

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	They are serching for a Mechanical Engineer who will beresponsible for the installation, maintenance, and optimization of mechanical systems and equipment within the pharmaceutical manufacturing facility.
	The role ensures efficient operation of production machinery, utilities, and support systems in compliance with safety standards and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) requirements.


Key Roles and Responsibilities


	Install, maintain, and troubleshoot mechanical equipment used in pharmaceutical production (e.g., mixers, granulators, compressors, HVAC systems).
	Ensure all mechanical systems operate efficiently to support uninterrupted production activities.
	Develop and implement preventive and corrective maintenance schedules.
	Monitor equipment performance and recommend improvements to enhance efficiency and reduce downtime.
	Ensure compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) and safety standards in all engineering activities.
	Perform routine inspections of machinery and utilities to identify potential faults.
	Collaborate with Production, Quality Assurance, and Quality Control teams to ensure equipment meets operational and quality requirements.
	Maintain accurate maintenance records, logs, and documentation.
	Supervise and guide technicians and maintenance staff.
	Ensure proper calibration and functionality of mechanical systems supporting production.
	Support installation and commissioning of new equipment and systems.


Qualifications and Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree (B.Eng./B.Sc.) in Mechanical Engineering or related field.
	3–7 years’ experience in mechanical engineering, preferably in pharmaceutical, FMCG, or manufacturing environment.
	Knowledge of industrial machinery, HVAC systems, and utility systems.
	Familiarity with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) and safety standards.
	Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.
	Good communication and teamwork abilities.
	Professional certification (e.g., COREN) is an added advantage.


Key Competencies:


	Equipment maintenance and reliability
	Troubleshooting and fault diagnosis
	Preventive maintenance planning
	Safety and compliance awareness
	Team collaboration
	Technical documentation.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Mechanical Engineer -Calabar" as the subjet of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "CRO Marketing Officer",
  "description": "RECORE is wholly owned by Nigerians, and was established with a vision to deliver needed solutions to companies that are seeking to improve their efficiency and profitability by focusing on their core business. Our outsourcing services cover Personnel Supply, Dispatch and Fleet and Facility Management Services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: CRO Marketing Officer

Job Locations:&nbsp;Abia, Abuja (FCT), Bauchi, Bayelsa, Benue, Cross River, Ekiti, Kaduna, Kano, Katsina, Kebbi, Kwara, Yobe and Zamfara
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Develop and execute CRO strategies, analyze user behavior, collaborate with teams, track KPIs, and stay updated on CRO trends.


Requirement


	Minimum of a BSc (2:2 or higher) in Marketing, business administration, or related field, NYSC completion, and digital marketing experience is an advantage.


Remuneration

CRO Marketing Officer Job at Recore Limited




        
    

        
        RECORE is wholly owned by Nigerians, and was established with a vision to deliver needed solutions to companies that are seeking to improve their efficiency and profitability by focusing on their core business. Our outsourcing services cover Personnel Supply, Dispatch and Fleet and Facility Management Services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: CRO Marketing Officer

Job Locations: Abia, Abuja (FCT), Bauchi, Bayelsa, Benue, Cross River, Ekiti, Kaduna, Kano, Katsina, Kebbi, Kwara, Yobe and Zamfara
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and execute CRO strategies, analyze user behavior, collaborate with teams, track KPIs, and stay updated on CRO trends.


Requirement


	Minimum of a BSc (2:2 or higher) in Marketing, business administration, or related field, NYSC completion, and digital marketing experience is an advantage.


Remuneration
N147,000 Gross / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "CRO Marketing [Preferred Location]" as the subject of the mail.
